payload.title: Every Java program has a main method
payload.body: Java needs a class and a main method to run. System.out.println() is how you print text. It looks long, but you'll memorize it fast!

payload.prompt: How do you print text in Java?
payload.explanation: System.out.println() prints a line of text in Java.

payload.prompt: Complete the print statement.
payload.explanation: println prints text and moves to the next line.

lessons.title: Types & Variables
lessons.summary: Declare variables with their type in Java.

payload.title: Java is a typed language
payload.body: In Java, you must say what type a variable is before naming it. int for whole numbers, double for decimals, String for text, boolean for true/false.

payload.prompt: Which type holds whole numbers in Java?
payload.explanation: int stores whole numbers like 0, 42, or -7.

payload.prompt: Declare a whole-number variable called lives with value 3.
payload.explanation: int is the type for whole numbers in Java.

payload.title: Strings hold text
payload.body: A String in Java is text surrounded by double quotes. You can join two strings with + — called concatenation.

payload.prompt: How do you join two Strings in Java?
payload.explanation: The + operator concatenates (joins) Strings in Java.

payload.prompt: Join the two strings with the correct operator.
payload.explanation: + joins (concatenates) strings in Java.

payload.title: Branching with if / else
payload.body: Java uses curly braces { } to group the code that runs when a condition is true. else runs when it's false.

payload.prompt: In Java, what wraps the condition in an if statement?
payload.explanation: The condition goes in ( ) and the code block goes in { }.

payload.prompt: Choose the correct comparison operator for 'greater than or equal to'.
payload.explanation: >= means 'greater than or equal to'.

lessons.title: For Loops
lessons.summary: Repeat code with a counting loop.

payload.title: The classic Java for loop
payload.body: Java's for loop has three parts: start, condition, step. It's used everywhere — from printing tables to processing lists.

payload.prompt: What does i++ mean in a Java for loop?
payload.explanation: i++ is shorthand for i = i + 1 — it increments i by 1 each iteration.

payload.prompt: Pick the correct step expression to count up by 1.
payload.explanation: i++ increments i by 1 after each loop iteration.

lessons.title: Methods
lessons.summary: Write a reusable method in Java.

payload.title: Methods are Java's functions
payload.body: A method is a named block of code you can call anytime. void means it doesn't return a value. static lets you call it from main without creating an object.

payload.prompt: What does void mean in a method definition?
payload.explanation: void means the method performs an action but doesn't send a value back.

payload.prompt: Choose the return type for a method that prints but returns nothing.
payload.explanation: void signals the method gives nothing back to the caller.

lessons.title: Arrays
lessons.summary: Store many values in one variable.

payload.title: Fixed-size lists
payload.body: A Java array holds a fixed number of values of the same type. Declare with type[] and access with [index].

payload.prompt: How do you get the number of items in a Java array?
payload.explanation: Arrays expose length as a field (no parentheses).

payload.prompt: Read the first element of scores.
payload.explanation: Square brackets with index 0.

lessons.title: Reading input
lessons.summary: Use Scanner to read from the keyboard.

payload.title: Scanner
payload.body: Scanner reads input from the user. nextLine() reads a line of text, nextInt() reads a whole number.

payload.prompt: Which method reads a line of text?
payload.explanation: nextLine() returns the rest of the current input line as a String.

payload.prompt: Read a whole number from the scanner.
payload.explanation: nextInt reads the next whole number.

payload.title: while (condition)
payload.body: A while loop keeps running its block as long as the condition is true. Great when you don't know how many times you'll loop.

payload.prompt: What happens if the condition is false on the very first check?
payload.explanation: while checks first, so the block may never execute.

payload.prompt: Loop until n is no longer greater than 0.
payload.explanation: > 0 keeps looping while n is still positive.

payload.title: A blueprint and an instance
payload.body: A class is a blueprint. An object is a real thing built from that blueprint, made with the new keyword.

payload.prompt: Which keyword builds a new object?
payload.explanation: new creates an instance of a class.

payload.prompt: Create a new Dog.
payload.explanation: new ClassName() calls the constructor.

lessons.title: Constructors
lessons.summary: Set up objects when they're built.

payload.title: Constructors run on new
payload.body: A constructor has the same name as its class and no return type. Use it to set fields when you create an object.

payload.prompt: What name does a constructor use?
payload.explanation: Constructor names always match the class name.

payload.prompt: Start a constructor for class Dog that takes a name.
payload.explanation: A constructor uses the class name and no return type.

lessons.title: Inheritance
lessons.summary: Build new classes from old ones.

payload.title: extends
payload.body: A subclass uses extends to inherit fields and methods from a parent class. It can then add or override behavior.

payload.prompt: Which keyword makes class B inherit from A?
payload.explanation: class B extends A.

payload.prompt: Make Cat inherit from Animal.
payload.explanation: extends chains the parent class.

lessons.title: ArrayList
lessons.summary: A growable list that's easier than arrays.

payload.title: Import and use
payload.body: ArrayList lives in java.util. Unlike arrays, you can add and remove items. Use generics like <String> to say what it holds.

payload.prompt: Which method adds an item to an ArrayList?
payload.explanation: ArrayList.add(x) appends x.

payload.prompt: Get the first name from the list.
payload.explanation: get(i) returns the item at index i.
